pattern: The same three-way capped split as before, but the totals are now too large to loop over even one child's share directly — inclusion-exclusion on the unbounded stars-and-bars count turns the capped count into a handful of closed-form terms.
hints: Without any cap, the number of ways to split a total among three children is a simple closed-form count; the cap only removes the cases where some child gets more than the limit.

Use inclusion-exclusion: start from the uncapped count, subtract the cases where at least one specific child exceeds the limit (by shifting that child's minimum share up past the limit), add back the cases where two children simultaneously exceed it, and subtract the case where all three do.

Each of those four terms is the same uncapped-count formula applied to a shifted total, so the whole answer is a fixed combination of four evaluations of one small helper — no loop over the totals is needed at all.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, larger limit than total
smallest possible total, largest possible limit
both n and limit at their maximum
total far exceeds three times the limit, so nothing fits
total equals exactly three times the limit, only the all-max split works
tiny limit that still permits a modest total to be split
total one short of the maximum feasible amount for the limit

stress.description: random totals and limits across the whole million-scale domain
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, min(size, 1000000))
    limit = rng.randint(1, min(size, 1000000))
    return [n, limit]

stress.description: total and limit both pinned near the shared feasibility boundary (3 * limit)
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    limit = rng.randint(1, min(size, 1000000))
    delta = rng.randint(-3, 3)
    n = max(1, min(1000000, 3 * limit + delta))
    return [n, limit]
